mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings expectations, driving S&P 500 forward returns positively.
failure_condition: If RSAFS growth is positive for 3 consecutive months but SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of such instances.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market, leading to reduced consumer spending and corporate profits, which negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over a 3-month period.
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises by more than 0.5pp over 3 months, but SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of such occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate is high and inflation (CPIAUCSL) is also accelerating, it indicates an aggressive monetary tightening stance by the central bank, which typically dampens economic activity and equity valuations.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS is above 4.0% and CPIAUCSL growth is above 3.0%, but SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in housing starts after a period of decline indicates renewed confidence in the housing market and broader economic recovery, boosting investor sentiment and S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST shows a month-over-month increase of at least 5% after a 3-month consecutive decline.
failure_condition: If HOUST increases by 5% MoM after a 3-month decline, but SPX 5-day returns are negative in more than 50% of such instances.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained expansion in industrial production reflects healthy manufacturing and supply-side activity, signaling robust economic growth and supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month average growth rate is above 0.2%.
failure_condition: If INDPRO 3-month average growth is above 0.2%, but SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 45% of triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Continuous job creation, as measured by non-farm payrolls, indicates a strong labor market and consumer base, which underpins corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a longer horizon.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS shows positive month-over-month growth for 6 consecutive months, but SPX 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of such periods.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth combined with accelerating retail sales indicates broad-based economic strength driven by both production and consumption, leading to higher corporate profits and positive equity market performance.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth > 2.5% AND RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5%.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 growth is above 2.5% and RSAFS growth is above 0.5%, but SPX 5-day returns are negative in more than 40% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A low and stable Federal Funds Rate, especially after a period of high rates, signals an accommodative monetary policy aimed at stimulating economic growth, which typically supports equity valuations.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS drops below 1.0% and remains there for 3 consecutive months.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS drops below 1.0% for 3 months, but SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of such periods.
